commands.review_summary:
The PR changes OpenCode Go fetching and snapshot conversion so missing Go subscription usage can fall back to Zen balance-only output, with focused parser/concurrency tests and an unreleased changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main fetches and parses Go subscription usage before optional Zen balance and has a test expecting Missing usage fields for a no-usage page. I did not run a live provider probe because AGENTS.md requires explicit permission for real account or browser-cookie access.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof from a Zen-only OpenCode setup, which automation cannot safely supply under the repository's live-provider restrictions.

target.title: fix(opencode): show Zen balance for Go accounts without subscription usage
target.body:
## Summary
- allow OpenCode Go accounts without Go subscription usage fields to fall back to the existing Zen balance fetch
- render Zen-only accounts as balance-only snapshots instead of false 0% rate windows
- reuse the Zen fallback when either the initial page precheck or final subscription parser rejects incomplete usage data
- wait for required fallback data through the caller's full timeout while keeping the 250 ms grace only for optional enrichment
- cancel the parallel balance task on unrelated failures and use synchronized request recording in concurrent tests
- keep the existing parse error when neither Go subscription usage nor Zen balance is available

Fixes #1476

## Tests
- `swift test --filter OpenCodeGoUsageFetcherErrorTests --skip-update` (16 tests passed)
- `make check` (SwiftFormat clean, SwiftLint 0 violations, packaging/release-path checks passed)
- `git diff --check`
- full-branch autoreview found two fallback gaps; both fixed
- follow-up autoreview clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Proof and risk
- one regression delays the Zen balance response beyond the optional 250 ms enrichment grace and verifies a balance-only snapshot still renders
- another regression exercises a page that passes the initial rolling-usage precheck but fails final weekly/reset parsing
- timeout assertions verify required fallback gets the caller's full timeout while successful subscription usage remains non-blocking
- no live account or browser-cookie probe was run; repository policy requires explicit permission for live provider probes that may access browser or Keychain state
- provider auth, cookie normalization, endpoints, and storage are unchanged
